Cynanchum is a genus of flowering plants that are commonly known as swallowworts, dogstrangle, or dogbane. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

The bioactive constituents of Cynanchum plants include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to their medicinal properties. These constituents have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and protecting against oxidative stress.

The most common herbal preparations made with Cynanchum plants include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, sore throat, and digestive issues.
